steps.title: Read for the signal
steps.content: The adverb “readily” signals that other researchers responded quickly and without resistance. The blank describes how they treated Nuttall’s work as groundbreaking — the key is that they accepted or recognized its status, and “recognition stemmed from her convincing demonstration.” The word must mean accepted or recognized.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — acknowledged: “Acknowledged” means recognized as having a certain status. Researchers readily recognized her work as groundbreaking based on its convincing demonstration — this fits exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — ensured: “Ensured” means guaranteed or made certain in advance. The researchers responded to published work they read; they did not contribute to or guarantee its quality beforehand. This word violates the timeline. ✗
  • Choice C — denied: “Denied” means refused to admit. The passage says recognition followed — not refusal — so this directly contradicts the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — underestimated: “Underestimated” means valued too little. The passage says researchers praised the work as groundbreaking, the opposite of undervaluing it. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
